items.rationale: Sees NFTs as a genuinely new asset class whose value comes from community, brand-building, programmable property rights, and online-offline utility — durable, not a fad. By late 2022 he committed to co-authoring a full book on them.
items.age_note: First framed in Nov-2021 HBR piece but continuously reinforced through 2022 (book deal Oct-2022, multiple a16z articles); current, not stale.

citations.quote: explaining why we think NFTs are real and here to stay
@NFTbark and I are writing a book about NFTs, to be published by @portfoliobooks

items.rationale: Believes building open platforms that share ownership and value with users creates more total value than walled-garden web2 — lowers cold-start friction, builds community network effects, and raises long-run consumer surplus. Joined a16z crypto research team to pursue this.

citations.quote: Web3 has the potential to unlock a more valuable internet for everyone.
design and user insight will continue to differentiate apps and marketplaces

items.rationale: His most recent thesis: the 2022 centralized blow-ups are an argument FOR decentralized, transparent, smart-contract-based finance — not against crypto. Most recent market statement in the corpus.
items.age_note: Stated Dec-2022, post-FTX — the freshest view; supersedes any earlier ambiguity about centralized crypto.

citations.quote: Do the collapses of FTX, Celsius, and Terra signal the end of crypto? No, but they do show why we need #DeFi.

items.rationale: Excited that giving away IP rights (Creative Commons Zero, 'Can't Be Evil' licenses) can grow a brand's value by inviting derivatives and co-creation, turning an adversarial game cooperative — while noting it isn't one-size-fits-all.

citations.quote: cc0 licensing is an incredibly compelling way to kickstart IP that may one day take on a life of its own.
@a16z crypto has just launched a suite of "Can't be Evil" licenses

items.rationale: Argues 'location, location, location' applies to scarce, ownable, portable metaverse spaces; value accrues to land on popular, interoperable platforms. Personally advising/holding generative-architecture projects (SOLIDS).

citations.quote: It matters which metaverse location you own!
My friend @0xfar has just launched something really cool: a generative architecture project called @SOLIDSNFT

items.rationale: As a market designer he's actively bullish on projects adopting clearing-price auctions over the descending pay-as-bid format most projects use; advising teams (Tally Labs, fine_digital) on auction mechanics.

citations.quote: SO BULLISH on @jenkinsthevalet @TallyLabsNFT running an actual clearing-price auction on-chain, rather than the descending pay-as-bid auctions so many projects have been using.
I generally prefer clearing-price auctions to descending pay-as-bid auctions

items.rationale: Defends creator royalties on social-welfare/incentive grounds and sees crypto wallets becoming integrated cross-platform reputation systems; recurring research and advisory theme.

citations.quote: we might want to give up some efficiency of allocation in exchange for providing a more direct reward to creators
people's full wallet is going to start being used as an integrated reputation system across platforms

items.rationale: Repeatedly and emphatically pushes back on framing NFTs as a sure way to make money; insists they are risky at any price point — his single most forceful market-risk warning.

citations.quote: BUYING NFTS AS INVESTMENTS IS INHERENTLY RISKY. At any price point. No investment is guaranteed to go up!
many NFT asset values fluctuate so widely that to the extent they are investments, they are emphatically not "risk-free."

items.rationale: Views the high sophistication needed to access crypto (and doubly NFTs), weak security and consumer protection, and poor wallet UX as first-order barriers to mainstream adoption.
items.age_note: Core articulation Nov-2021, but theme recurs through 2022 (wallet UX, verification, Coinbase onboarding); persisted and current.

citations.quote: even gaining access to crypto requires a high degree of sophistication -- and newer products like NFTs doubly or triply so!
NFTs face many of the current general challenges of crypto -- especially in terms of environmental costs, and lack of security/consumer protection.

items.rationale: Frames the FTX/Celsius/Terra collapses as failures of centralized intermediaries, the implicit counterpoint to his DeFi enthusiasm; most recent risk theme.

citations.quote: Do the collapses of FTX, Celsius, and Terra signal the end of crypto? No, but they do show why we need #DeFi.

items.rationale: Frequently flags compromised Discords, impersonation/scam accounts, phishing mint links, and rugs as a persistent harm — including criticizing platforms for boosting scam ads while throttling legitimate projects.

citations.quote: it's appalling that you're letting these scam accounts signal-boost
a link to a fake mint site is being pushed repeatedly. DO NOT ATTEMPT TO MINT from the provided link.

items.rationale: Concerned astronomical Ethereum transaction costs and congestion (a tragedy-of-the-commons across competing mints) harm NFT projects and pricing; hopeful tech/infrastructure fixes are coming.
items.age_note: Largely a 2021 concern; partially mitigated by his enthusiasm for the Sept-2022 Merge and L2s — softer by year-end but never retracted.

citations.quote: because many of them run on the ethereum blockchain, they're also subject to astronomical transaction costs.
the current congestion is harming everyone, but it's a tragedy of the commons.

items.rationale: Holds that NFT value is community-created, so toxic or fading communities are an existential project risk regardless of art quality — a network-goods failure mode.
items.age_note: Endorsed-quote (Aug-2021) but the underlying community-value thesis is restated throughout his 2022 writing; not stale.

citations.quote: if the community is toxic, [the product is] dead on arrival.
Lack of engagement can then become a self-fulfilling prophecy, devaluing the NFTs themselves.

items.rationale: Listed environmental cost as a core crypto challenge needing infrastructure-level change for the space to be sustainable.
items.age_note: Emphasized in 2021; the Sept-2022 Ethereum Merge (PoW→PoS) he celebrated likely defused much of this for him — concern stated but not refreshed in 2022.

citations.quote: We need a lot of tech changes at the infrastructure level in order to make all of this sustainable.

items.rationale: Treats ETH less as a price bet than as the foundational infrastructure under NFTs/web3; holds it and celebrated the Merge as a landmark technical achievement. Enthusiasm is for the platform, not speculative upside.
items.age_note: Note he also flags ETH/NFT price tension — bullish on the chain, neutral on ETH as a trade.

citations.quote: GM! Happy Merge Day to all who celebrate, QED
If "1 $ETH = 1 $ETH," then why do #NFT prices fall when Ethereum is on a bull run?

items.rationale: Endorses the view that SHIB lacks fundamental economic basis — a meme without a reason to thrive — emblematic of his skepticism toward fundamentals-free meme tokens.
items.age_note: Stated Nov-2021 and not restated; carried forward as his standing view on meme coins.

citations.quote: its provenance as a meme leaves it without much of a fundamental economic reason to thrive. Fair.

items.rationale: Treated the $SOS airdrop hype dismissively, as froth driven by social-media right-click-save mimicry rather than value.

citations.quote: right-click-save messages saying things like "I got a billion kajillion $SOS"

risk_regime.rationale: Kominers stayed structurally bullish on crypto/web3/NFTs through the entire 2022 bear market and the FTX collapse, reframing failures as reasons to build (DeFi, better market design) rather than retreat. His risk-on posture is toward the technology and its market-design value over the long run — paired with explicit, repeated caution that the market is 'really, really early,' individual NFT buys are risky at any price, and accessibility/security/scams are unsolved. So: long-run risk-on, near-term cautious and disclosure-disciplined.

notes: Kominers is an academic/advisor (HBS professor; joined a16z crypto research team Apr-2022), not a trader — his 'market view' is about technology and market-design value, not price calls, and he scrupulously discloses holdings/advisory roles (RealSupDucks, AdamBombSquad, chain_runners, HungryWolvesNFT, thingdoms, fine_digital, TallyLabs, etc.). The vast majority of the corpus is non-market (daily Wordle, Bloomberg 'Conundrums' puzzles, number theory/date numerology, and extensive COVID-policy criticism) and was excluded. Recency weighting: NFT-value and web3 theses originate in 2021 HBR pieces but are continuously reinforced through 2022 (book deal Oct-2022, numerous a16z articles), so treated as grounded/current rather than persisted. The Sept-2022 Ethereum Merge appears to have softened his earlier environmental-cost and gas-fee concerns. His clearest forward stance at year-end (Dec-2022, post-FTX): crypto isn't dead, and the path forward is decentralization plus better market/IP design. Other tokens he interacts with (VOLT, APE) reflect community participation rather than investment conviction and were omitted.
